scope: same-start whole-rewrite versus defect-scoped revision A/B for Chapter 3
model_output_allowance_policy: request each endpoint maximum; do not cap reasoning, time, or review depth
baseline_boundary: dfedb4c — run-005 closed; exact R3 and review frozen; no shared factory asset changed

experiment.single_variable: revision scope after a fresh review identifies local defects in an otherwise substantially correct artifact
experiment.shared_objective: produce the strongest complete Chapter 3; repair every R3 blocker and measured craft weakness without adding unsupported claims, losing assigned content, or changing the chapter job
experiment.common_commission_exact: Produce the strongest complete final-book Chapter 3 from the frozen materials. Repair every defect and quality note in the latest review. Maintain the chapter's one assigned job, exact mantras and instruction, evidence limits, continuity, and original expression; add no argument, evidence, or promise not licensed by the plan. Use your full judgment and do not narrate your process. Return only the complete chapter, beginning with Chapter 3.
experiment.arm_a_scope_exact: Treat this as a complete rewrite of the artifact. You may recast any passage as your judgment requires.
experiment.arm_b_scope_exact: Treat this as an edit of the existing artifact. Preserve what already works and its unaffected meaning; revise as much as your judgment requires to repair the review, including the measured craft surface.
experiment.arm_a_control: whole-artifact writer may rewrite any passage
experiment.arm_b_treatment: defect-scoped editor preserves unaffected meaning and changes defect-bearing passages plus the measured craft surface
experiment.generation_order: both arms complete before any arm review is opened
experiment.measurement: the same blind model-diverse top-reasoning panel reviews each arm independently; panel composition is symmetric measurement, not the tested lever
experiment.arm_acceptance: convergent ACCEPT from both independent reviewers

causal_decision_rule.supported: B convergently ACCEPT and A does not
causal_decision_rule.refuted: A convergently ACCEPT and B fails because scope preservation prevented a necessary repair
causal_decision_rule.inconclusive: both accept, both fail, or reviewers materially disagree

experiment.product_selection: advance any sole convergently accepted arm; if both accept, use a fresh blind pairwise quality preference and make no revision-scope causal claim

held_constant: exact R3 candidate and exact R3 review
shared craft objective and all blocking requirements
review inputs, reviewer panel, rubrics, and acceptance rule
no time, token, or reasoning cap on reviewers

blocking_groups: premature mechanism diagnosis and job drift

direction_audit: GO — isolate revision scope; use diverse reviewers only as blinded measurement; do not add research architecture or a chapter-specific shared prompt
checkpoint_direction_audit: NO-GO on arm promotion, shared-prompt change, retroactive tie-break, or another A/B; conditional GO on H-044 adversarial review coverage followed by one unchanged-loop repair and fresh Sol/Grok convergence
factory_promotion_boundary: even a supported one-chapter result remains provisional until replicated on another chapter and then survives the zero-amendment Stage C subject holdout

result.h043_causal_verdict: INCONCLUSIVE — both arms failed convergence and both reviewer pairs materially disagreed

notes: Both Opus arms completed concurrently before either artifact was opened for review. The symmetric panel then split identically on both arms: Sol found unbounded installation and premature mechanism claims; Gemini accepted while materially undercounting each artifact. Neither arm met the preregistered convergence gate, no live chapter changed, and no shared factory rule was promoted.
